data.title: Bank of Communications Posts 'Positive' Growth in Q1 Net Profit
data.description:
Bank of Communications achieved "positive" year-on-year growth in both net profit and net interest income in the first quarter, according to a Tuesday filing with the Hong Kong bourse.

The lender's balance of loans increased by more than 360 billion yuan as of the end of March compared with the end of December 2024, the filing said.

data.title: 7-Eleven Owner Posts Higher Quarterly Net Profit on Asset-Sales Gains
data.description:
By Kosaku Narioka

Seven & i Holdings reported a rise in quarterly net profit as gains from asset sales helped offset weaker earnings from convenience-store operations at home and abroad, underscoring the challenges the 7-Eleven parent faces as it tries to stave off a takeover attempt by the Canadian owner of Circle K.

The Japanese owner of 7-Eleven said Wednesday that net profit more than doubled from a year earlier to 109.44 billion yen, equivalent to $748.1 million, for the three months ended Feb. 28. That beat the estimate of Y98.5 billion in a poll of analysts by data provider Visible Alpha.

Fourth-quarter revenue edged 0.4% higher to Y2.903 trillion.

Operating profit declined for both its domestic and overseas convenience-store businesses.

For the year that began in March, it projected revenue to decrease 10% to Y10.761 trillion and net profit to climb 47% to Y255.00 billion.

Seven & i announced a series of measures last month to boost its shareholder value as it sought to head off a takeover bid by Canada's Alimentation Couche-Tard.

The company named its first American chief executive, former Walmart executive Stephen Hayes Dacus, and said it planned to list its North American convenience-store business. The company also agreed to sell a group of superstores and other businesses to U.S. private-equity firm Bain Capital for $5.4 billion and said it would buy back up to $13 billion of its own shares, using proceeds from the sale of the businesses as well as from the initial public offering.

The 7-Eleven owner in September rejected an initial $39 billion buyout bid from Couche-Tard, saying the proposal underestimated the company's value. Couche-Tard later raised its offer to about $47 billion and in January submitted a revised, yen-denominated proposal at Seven & i's request.

Write to Kosaku Narioka at kosaku.narioka@wsj.com

data.title: Tietoevry Unit Continues Cloud, Infrastructure Support for Outokumpu
data.description:
Tietoevry's Tietoevry Tech Services received a new contract to continue developing and securing Outokumpu's digital infrastructure.

Under the new five-year deal, the Finnish IT services company will provide critical infrastructure services, including management of SAP platform and Microsoft Azure public cloud services, according to a Wednesday release.

data.title: DAX Resumes Decline as Reciprocal Tariffs Take Effect
data.description:
Frankfurt's DAX opened 2.5% lower around 19.790 on Wednesday, as Europe's market sell-off resumed with investors reacting to the overnight rollout of sweeping US tariffs, including a 20% levy on EU imports.

The escalating trade tensions are increasing worries about a potential recession and boosting demand for safe-haven assets.

On the domestic front, talks between the conservatives and the Social Democrats are to resume this morning after long negotiations on Tuesday.

Negotiators said that a coalition agreement is likely to be announced by the afternoon.

data.title: Rusta’s climate targets approved by the Science Based Targets initiative
data.description:
Rusta is proud to announce that its science-based targets for achieving net-zero greenhouse gas emissions across the value chain by 2045 have been approved by the Science Based Targets initiative (SBTi). Along with the approval of the overall net-zero target, SBTi has also validated Rusta’s near-term and long-term targets. Rusta formally committed to SBTi in October 2024.

In October 2024, Rusta committed to implement emission-reduction targets in line with SBTi, both within Rusta’s own operations and throughout the value chain. Rusta’s commitment to SBTi reflects the company’s broader ambition to drive meaningful progress and address the climate crisis. Through this initiative, Rusta aims to further strengthen its sustainability ambitions.

Rusta’s science-based targets for achieving net-zero greenhouse gas emissions across the value chain by 2045 have now been approved by the SBTi. The approved science-based targets are as follows:

Overall Net-Zero Target: Rusta commits to reach net-zero greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions across the value chain by FY2045.

Near-Term Targets: Rusta commits to reduce absolute scope 1 and 2 GHG emissions 100% by FY2030 from a FY2023 base year. Rusta also commits to increase active annual sourcing of renewable electricity from 84% in FY2023 to 100% by FY2030. Rusta further commits to reduce scope 3 GHG emissions from purchased goods and upstream transportation and distribution 64% per million SEK value added by FY2034 from a FY2023 base year.

Long-Term Targets: Rusta commits to maintain 100% absolute scope 1 and 2 GHG emission reductions from FY2030 through FY2045 from a FY2023 base year. Rusta also commits to reduce scope 3 GHG emissions from purchased goods, upstream transportation and distribution and use of sold products 97% per million SEK value added within the same timeframe.

“Having our climate targets approved by SBTi is an important milestone in Rusta’s endeavor to be the most sustainable brand within our sector. The validation reinforces our commitment to reducing emissions and actively working to limit global warming in line with the Paris Agreement goals. With the approved targets, we remain dedicated to address the climate crisis while further strengthening our position as a company that actively contributes to a better future,” says Jesper Bondesson, Sustainability Manager at Rusta.

Read more about Science Based Targets here.

Read more about Rusta’s sustainability initiatives here.

For further information, please contact:Cecilia Gärdestad, Corporate PR and Investor RelationsPhone: +46 (0)70 166 48 73E-mail: cecilia.gardestad@rusta.cominvestors.rusta.com

About Science Based Targets initiativeThe Science Based Targets initiative (SBTi) is a corporate climate action organization that enables companies and financial institutions worldwide to play their part in combating the climate crisis. SBTi develops standards, tools and guidance which allow companies to set greenhouse gas emissions reductions targets in line with what is needed to keep global heating below catastrophic levels and reach net-zero by 2050 at latest. The SBTi is incorporated as a charity, with a subsidiary which will host the target validation services. SBTi’s partners are CDP, the United Nations Global Compact, the We Mean Business Coalition, the World Resources Institute (WRI), and the World Wide Fund for Nature (WWF). sciencebasedtargets.org

About RustaRusta is a leading retailer in the Nordic low-price market with over 220 stores in Sweden, Norway, Finland and Germany. The physical stores are supplemented by Rusta's online sales channel, Rusta Online, in Sweden and Finland. Rusta offers a wide but carefully selected range of home and leisure products, with good quality at low prices. Rusta has more than 4,800 employees with headquarter in Upplands Väsby, Sweden. Rusta's shares are traded on Nasdaq Stockholm (RUSTA). investors.rusta.com

data.title: The CAC 40 Index Opens 2.33% Lower
data.description:
In Paris, the CAC 40 Index is falling 166 points.

Losses were driven by Sanofi (-4.87%), STMicroelectronics (-4.51%) and Unibail Rodamco (-3.71%).

data.title: NRG Energy Keeps Dividend at $0.44 per Share, Payable May 15 to Shareholders as of May 1
Polaris' Taps CEO as Chairman
data.description:
Polaris Group said Chen Hung-Wen has resigned as the company chairman on Tuesday.

His role will be taken over by the healthcare consulting company's chief executive officer Hsu Jaan-Pyng, according to a Tuesday Taiwan Exchange filing.

data.title: Tesco's Premium and Value Segments Are StrongMarket Talk
data.description: Tesco is making important moves into the premium segment while keeping prices competitive, Third Bridge analyst Orwa Mohamad says in a note. The performance of the U.K. grocer's 'Finest' range is robust and continues to gain traction with shoppers deciding to pay more for better quality, Mohamad says. "This shows Tesco's ability to win at both ends of the market--offering great value through Clubcard and Aldi Price Match, while also delivering a premium experience that competitors struggle to match," the analyst says. Any cautious guidance from Tesco ahead could be related to cost pressures but not underlying weakness, given the positive Christmas performance and start to 2025, he says. Tesco will report its fiscal 2025 performance on Thursday. Shares are down 1.5%. (michael.susin@wsj.com)

data.title: Global Equities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Equities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

0731 GMT - Tesco is making important moves into the premium segment while keeping prices competitive, Third Bridge analyst Orwa Mohamad says in a note. The performance of the U.K. grocer's 'Finest' range is robust and continues to gain traction with shoppers deciding to pay more for better quality, Mohamad says. "This shows Tesco's ability to win at both ends of the market--offering great value through Clubcard and Aldi Price Match, while also delivering a premium experience that competitors struggle to match," the analyst says. Any cautious guidance from Tesco ahead could be related to cost pressures but not underlying weakness, given the positive Christmas performance and start to 2025, he says. Tesco will report its fiscal 2025 performance on Thursday. Shares are down 1.5%. (michael.susin@wsj.com)

0720 GMT - Tesco has a robust position to face Asda's turnaround plans and a potential renewed price war to take market share, Third Bridge analyst Orwa Mohamad says in a note. The largest U.K. grocer by market share has already highly competitive pricing and the gap with discounters has materially narrowed over the past year in many categories, the analyst adds. Tesco's robust scale, robust supplier ties, and its loyalty program provide tools to protect market share without hurting profit margins, Mohamad says. "Asda's turnaround plan has made headlines, but experts aren't convinced it can reclaim share just through lower prices," he adds. The analysts says the market selloff of food retailers shares on Asda's news is believed to be overly pessimistic. Tesco's stock is down nearly 9% since the start of 2025 but up nearly 20% over the last 52 weeks. (michael.susin@wsj.com)

0720 GMT - Tesco has a robust position to face Asda's turnaround plans and a potential renewed price war to take market share, Third Bridge analyst Orwa Mohamad says in a note. The largest U.K. grocer by market share has already highly competitive pricing and the gap with discounters has materially narrowed over the past year in many categories, the analyst adds. Tesco's robust scale, robust supplier ties, and its loyalty program provide tools to protect market share without hurting profit margins, Mohamad says. "Asda's turnaround plan has made headlines, but experts aren't convinced it can reclaim share just through lower prices," he adds. The analysts says the market selloff of food retailers shares on Asda's news is believed to be overly pessimistic. Tesco's stock is down nearly 9% since the start of 2025 but up nearly 20% over the last 52 weeks. (michael.susin@wsj.com)

0723 GMT - Pharma stocks take a hit in early morning European trade after U.S. President Trump said at a Tuesday dinner with House Republicans that tariffs on pharmaceuticals will be imposed very shortly. British AstraZeneca and GSK slump 3.9% and 2.8%, respectively. Swiss Roche and Novartis fall 4.8% and 4.1%, respectively. French Sanofi is down 4.5%. (helena.smolak@wsj.com)

0719 GMT - PageGroup's outlook seems unclear due to increased market uncertainty following the introduction of U.S. tariffs, RBC Capital Markets analysts Karl Green and Andrew Brooke say. The recruiter hasn't given any guidance, the analysts note. Though the company has started a cost-savings plan--which will deliver around 15 million pounds in savings in 2026--it will also result in an EBIT hit of around 10 million pounds this year, they add. "We continue to see strong total shareholder return potential over the medium-term for patient investors given the material potential upside to our mid-cycle target and the potential returns from the strong balance sheet, albeit clearly the short-term outlook remains murky," they say. Shares are down 3.8% at 247 pence. (anthony.orunagoriainoff@dowjones.com)

0709 GMT - South Korea's benchmark Kospi fell 1.7% to close at 2293.70, dragged by semiconductor and battery stocks. Foreign and institutional investors remained net sellers as U.S. tariff concerns continued weighing on sentiment. The country announced emergency support measures for its auto sector to ease the tariff impact but couldn't reverse the overall downbeat mood. EV battery maker Samsung SDI fell 2.6% after cutting its pricing of new shares by 14% for a rights issue amid its stock plunge. Memory-chip maker SK Hynix lost 2.7%. USD/KRW settled 0.7% higher at 1,484.10 in Seoul onshore trading. South Korea's 10-year government bond yield is up 3.7 bps at 2.723%. (kwanwoo.jun@wsj.com)

0701 GMT - Australia-based biotech company CSL, which collects plasma in the U.S. and manufactures many plasma-based products at plants in Europe, would likely need to raise prices by between 4% to 5.1% to offset Trump's planned tariffs on pharmaceuticals, say Jefferies analysts. While it isn't clear exactly how the Trump pharmaceutical tariffs will work, the good news for CSL is that over time it may be able to move plasma product manufacturing from high tariff locations in Europe to lower tariff areas like Australia, say David Stanton and Vanessa Thomson. This will put it in a slightly better position than its major European competitors. But in general, "plasma companies may see difficulties transferring complex, expensive operations at plants that can take years to build," they add. CSL shares ended 5% lower. (mike.cherney@wsj.com; @Mike_Cherney)

0659 GMT - Santander's strategy review seems to be gathering momentum, Citi says in a research note after Bloomberg reported that the Spanish bank is exploring options for its Polish operations. The news follows reports earlier this year by the Financial Times that Santander is considering exiting the U.K. market, among a number of strategic options. "The rationale of exiting Poland seems less compelling than selling, for instance, the U.K. bank," analysts Marta Sanchez Romero and Anand Demble write. The profitability of its Polish unit--with a 15% return on equity for 2024--is almost double the U.K.'s 8% and above the group's 13%, they say. However, the Polish banking market is a highly competitive one in which Santander has been losing market share in deposits, they add. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

0653 GMT - Nordic markets are seen opening lower, with IG calling the OMXS30 down 2.8% at around 2175. The U.S. announced additional import tariffs of 50% on China from today, meaning total tariffs on U.S. imports from China are now at 104%, SEB analysts say in a note. "The trade conflict looks set to escalate and the question now is whether more countries will end up in a similar spiral." Stock markets in Europe had a positive day Tuesday as they had closed before Trump's announcement of increased tariffs against China. U.S. stock markets fell as a result of the trade turmoil. Today, the country-specific reciprocal tariffs that were presented a week ago have taken effect. Stock markets in Japan and Hong Kong have fallen overnight and futures in both Europe and the U.S. are currently glowing red. OMXS30 closed at 2237.25, OMXN40 at 2136.93 and OBX at 1332.19. (dominic.chopping@wsj.com)

0646 GMT - The current market valuations of Santander and Santander Polska provide an opportunity to free up capital that could be reinvested to prop up returns elsewhere, Citi says in a research note after Bloomberg reported that the bank is exploring options to sell its 62% stake in its Polish operations. Santander Polska's shares trade at an around 60% premium to book value, while the wider Santander group trades at a 14% premium, analysts Marta Sanchez Romero and Anand Demble write. "The Polish banking market remains highly competitive and Santander (third largest bank in the country) has been losing market share in deposits over the past few years," they say. Making better use of parts of its operations could help the Spanish lender improve its valuation, they add. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

0646 GMT - European stocks are expected to open sharply lower as worries about tariffs and the impact on the global economy return to haunt markets. A rebound in equities on Tuesday proved brief as U.S. President Trump's sweeping duties, including a 104% tariff on Chinese goods, took effect Wednesday. Germany's DAX index and France's CAC 40 are expected to open 3.5% lower, while the U.K.'s FTSE 100 index is seen opening down 2.4%, according to IG. "What looked like a rebound day for equities was...capped by new tariff threats from Trump and zero signs of China backing down," analysts at Danske Bank say in a note. (jessica.fleetham@wsj.com)

0644 GMT - Japan's Nikkei Stock Average fell 3.9% to close at 31714.03, tracking Wall Street's losses overnight amid growing worries over U.S. tariffs. "Hopes of seeing (President) Trump roll back tariffs before they go live were dashed," Swissquote Bank's Ipek Ozkardeskaya says in an email. Sentiment across global financial markets were also dashed, the senior analyst adds. Electronics- and technology-related stocks led declines. Yaskawa Electric slipped 8.85%, Lasertec fell 8.5%, Sharp dropped 8.3% and Disco Corp. shed 8.2%. USD/JPY was at 145.38, down sharply from 147.50 as of Tuesday's Tokyo stock market close. JGB 10-year yield was 2bps higher at 1.275%. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

data.description:
In Frankfurt, the DAX Index is losing 428 points.

data.title: REG - Eesti Energia AS - Update on Eesti Energia credit rating by Moody’s
data.description:
RNS Number : 2872E Eesti Energia AS 09 April 2025  

Update on Eesti Energia credit rating by Moody's

On 8 April 2025, Moody's has affirmed Eesti Energia AS credit rating Baa3, but changes the outlook to negative from stable. The negative outlook reflects that the voluntary takeover bid that Eesti Energia has made to acquire the shares of Enefit Green will mean an increase in financial leverage.

Details on the publication can be found here: https://www.enefit.com/en/ettevottest/investorile

Further Information:

data.title: Accor, InterGlobe Partner to Build Network of 300 Hotels in India
data.description:
By Pierre Bertrand

France's Accor and Indian travel conglomerate InterGlobe Enterprises aim to build a network of 300 hotels in India over the next five years as they seek to create the country's fastest-growing hospitality company.

The companies said they would bring together their currently owned assets, development and management businesses in the country in a bid to expand in India's hospitality market.

"This new entity will become the exclusive vehicle for growing all Accor brands in India, including luxury and lifestyle brands from Ennismore, Accor's fast-growing hospitality portfolio," the companies said Wednesday.

Hospitality company Accor will continue to lead operations, brand management and provide full access to its brands and services, they said.

As part of the partnership, the companies said they would jointly invest in and become the largest shareholder in Treebo, an Indian company that manages 800 hotels across 120 cities.

In addition, the companies said that Treebo would lead the development of the Ibis and Mercure brands in India through a license agreement.

The companies said that the combined portfolio of Accor and Treebo would create India's third-largest hospitality player, with more than 30,000 rooms.

Write to Pierre Bertrand at pierre.bertrand@wsj.com

data.title: EverFocus Electronics' Revenue Rises 27% in March
data.description:
EverFocus Electronics' revenue in March jumped 27.2% year on year to NT$35.8 million, according to a Tuesday Taiwan Exchange filing.

For the first quarter, the company's revenue grew 7.06% year on year to NT$88.0 million, the image processors developer said.

data.title: Hang Seng Tech Index Rises Further; Last 2.3% Higher at 4672.18
U.S. tariffs to lead Spain's Central Bank to lower economic growth forecasts
data.description: The impact of U.S. tariffs on Spain will lead the Bank of Spain to revise down its growth forecasts, Spain's central bank governor and European Central Bank's policymaker Jose Luis Escriva said on Wednesday.

data.title: Market Chatter: Vietnam to Intensify US Tariff Talks with a 'No Fear' Approach, Says PM
data.description:
Vietnam plans to step up its negotiations with the US on recent 46% tariffs, while keeping a calm and measured approach, VN Express reported, citing Prime Minister Pham Minh Chinh.

While acknowledging the impact of the tariff, Chinh urged that the focus should be on dialogue rather than confrontation. He added that the challenges posed by the new tariffs were not as severe as those Vietnam had faced during the early "Doi Moi" reforms or the war period.

The prime minister suggested that this could be an opportunity for Vietnam to diversify its markets and products. Vietnam would also seek to leverage free trade agreements and explore new global partnerships, the report said.

Disclaimer

Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.

data.title: Xplora Technologies AS: Monthly Subscriptions Update – March 2025
data.description:
As of March 31, 2025, Xplora Technologies has 358k subscriptions, an increase of 103k from March 2024 (255k).

This month’s subscription update reflects Xplora Technologies’ existing business operations.

This month’s highlight:

- 11k new subscriptions (gross)

This quarter’s highlight:

- 33k new subscriptions (gross), up 14% from 29k in Q1 24

Note: We expect our churn rate to be consistent with historic numbers. Subscriptions encompass connectivity plans (mobile subscriptions), premium services, B2B service revenues, and service fees for Xplora smartwatches without Xplora mobile subscriptions (Nordics). Xplora has seasonal changes, especially related to Back-to-school and the Festive Season. The net growth in the subscription base will therefore fluctuate throughout the year.

For further information please contact:

Sten Kirkbak, CEO, +47 92203710, sten.kirkbak@xplora.com

Knut Stålen, CFO, +47 92043458, knut.stalen@xplora.com

About Xplora Technologies AS:

Xplora is a platform and services company and an industry leader in the market for children’s smartwatches. Xplora was founded to give children a safe onboarding to the digital life and a better balance between screen time and physical activity. Xplora’s vision is to enable children around the world to experience how their everyday activities can create value and make a positive change in the world. The company is headquartered in Norway with operations in leading European markets. Xplora is listed on Euronext Growth under the ticker symbol XPLRA.

data.title: Adidas, Barclays Bank, Burberry
data.description:
Securities analysts revised their ratings and price targets on several European companies, including Adidas, Barclays Bank, and Burberry on Wednesday.

HIGHLIGHTS

  • * Adidas (ADSGN.DE): HSBC cuts target price to EUR 240 from EUR 260

  • * Barclays Bank : Morgan Stanley cuts target price to 350p from 375p

  • * Burberry Group : Deutsche Bank cuts target price to 900p from 1400p

  • * Jupiter Fund Management : Morgan Stanley cuts to underweight from equal weight

  • * Hermes : Deutsche Bank raises target price to EUR 2550 from EUR 2220

Following is a summary of research actions on European companies reported by Reuters on Wednesday. Stock entries are in alphabetical order.

data.title: Hong Kong Pharma Stocks Decline as Trump Threatens Tariffs on Imported Drugs
data.description:
Hong Kong pharmaceutical stocks fell on Wednesday after US President Donald Trump said his administration would soon implement "a major tariff on pharmaceuticals" from foreign nations.

Trump made the remarks at a Republican event, suggesting the move will force drug companies to relocate their operations to the US, according to a USA Today report.

While initially sparing pharmaceuticals from recent tariffs, Trump has indicated for months that the imported drug industry is a future target, the report said.

The news sent shares of Hong Kong-listed drugmakers falling on Wednesday afternoon.

Alphamab Oncology led the decliners with an almost 6% fall, followed by Boan Biotech with a 5% drop. Luye Pharma slid 4.4%, while BeiGene and Genscript Bio retreated nearly 4%. Zai Lab slipped 3%.

data.title: Montenegro hires Put-Inzenjering for Pljevlja-Niksic road design
data.description:
PODGORICA (Montenegro), April 9 (SeeNews) - Montenegro's state-run motorway operator Monteput said it has signed a 159,000 euro ($174,000) deal with Podgorica-based Put-Inzenjering to draw the conceptual design study for a planned 96-km Niksic-Zabljak-Plevlja high-speed road.

The conceptual design should identify the optimal route and create conditions for preparing the remaining documents necessary for the project launch. The design study should be completed in six months, Monteput said in a statement on Tuesday when the contract was signed.

The planned high-speed road will connect Montenegro's second-largest city of Niksic, in the west, to the third-largest city of Pljevlja, in the country's north. The road is divided into three sections - Niksic-Savnik, Savnik-Zabljak and Zabljak-Pljevlja, as per the tender documents.

In August, prime minister Milojko Spajic said that the road is planned to have four lanes and to include the construction of a new bridge over the Tara River, avoiding the road curves around the town of Savnik and the Tara canyon. At present, the 130 km trip from Niksic to Pljevlja takes two and a half hours.

In February, Monteput said that Put-Inzenjering had submitted the best bid in the tender for the Niksic-Pljevlja road design, noting that the only other bidder in the tender - a consortium of Montenegrin firm Decom Group and Belgrade-based Louis Berger doo, was disqualified.

Put-Inzenjering was established in August 2002. It is 100%-owned by its founder and executive director Radenko Ostojic, according to the Montenegrin business registry.

($ = 0.913 euro)

data.title: Toluene prices inch higher in Asia
data.description: On Tuesday, toluene prices gained in Asia. An industry source in Asia informed a Polymerupdate team member, "Prices trended upward on the back of bullish downstream benzene values coupled with healthy buying sentiments in the Asian market."FOB Korea toluene prices on Tuesday were assessed at the USD 610-620/mt levels, up USD (+10/mt) from Monday's assessed levels.FOB China toluene prices on Tuesday were assessed at the USD 670-680/mt levels, higher by USD (+10/mt) from Monday.Meanwhile, downstream benzene prices on Tuesday were assessed at the USD 715-725/mt FOB Korea levels, a day on day gain of USD (+15/mt).

data.title: Fresh General purpose polystyrene (GPPS) offers in Afghanistan
data.description: A Polymerupdate source in Afghanistan reported that an Iranian trader is heard to have offered its GPPS Injection grade (Iran-origin) at the USD 1185/mt levels.This offer is for April 2025 delivery on FCA basis.

data.title: RTX's Collins Aerospace Wins 6-Year Renewal of Contract With Heathrow Airport
data.description:
RTX's Collins Aerospace division said Wednesday it entered a six-year renewal of its contract with Heathrow Airline Operators' Committee to deliver a common-use passenger processing system.

Under the deal, the company will continue offering its ARINC cMUSE platform for all four London-Heathrow Airport passenger terminals.

data.title: MANDATORY NOTIFICATION OF TRADE BY PRIMARY INSIDERS
data.description:
On 8 April 2025, Ms. Yee Man Gloria, WONG, a close associate of Mr. Siu FaiNG,chairman and director of Jinhui Shipping and Transportation Limited (the'Company'), has purchased 59,794 shares of the Company in the Oslo StockExchange at an average price of NOK5.1601 per share. Following to the abovepurchase, Ms. Yee Man Gloria, WONG holds 1,212,989 shares of the Company.

This information is subject of the disclosure requirements pursuant to article19 of the EU Market Abuse Regulation (MAR) and section 5-12 of the NorwegianSecurities Trading Act.

9 April 2025
